title: SentinelOne EDR Setup Guide
content:
SentinelOne EDR Setup Guide

No Action Required: SentinelOne is automatically installed by Jamf MDM and you do not need to perform any installation tasks.

No Action Required: SentinelOne is automatically installed by Jamf MDM and you do not need to perform any installation tasks.

If you are using Advanced Intrusion Detection Environment (AIDE) to monitor file integrity and detect intrusions, you will need to create an exclusion in AIDE. When both AIDE and the SentinelOne Agent are running together, AIDE is not able to update its database correctly. AIDE tries to scan a SentinelOne Directory and cannot scan it.

To create an exclusion for SentinelOne, edit /etc/aide.conf and add !/opt/sentinelone/mount to the end to ignore the SentinelOne Agent mount directory:

These steps are only needed for Linux laptops. SentinelOne is already automatically installed on macOS by Jamf MDM.

These steps are only needed for Linux laptops. SentinelOne is already automatically installed on macOS by Jamf MDM.

Make sure you are using the approved Linux distribution (Ubuntu LTS). See the Approved Operating Systems page for details.

Make sure you are using the approved Linux distribution (Ubuntu LTS). See the Approved Operating Systems page for details.

Download the configuration file and the installer (DEB/RPM).

Download the configuration file and the installer (DEB/RPM).

In the following examples, we’ll assume you put those in the following locations and that you renamed the package name to be sentinelagent . Configuration file: ~/.config/sentinelone/config.cfg DEB package: ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.deb RPM package: ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.rpm

In the following examples, we’ll assume you put those in the following locations and that you renamed the package name to be sentinelagent .

Configuration file: ~/.config/sentinelone/config.cfg DEB package: ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.deb RPM package: ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.rpm

Configuration file: ~/.config/sentinelone/config.cfg DEB package: ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.deb RPM package: ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.rpm

Get your laptop’s serial number. sudo dmidecode -s system-serial-number

Get your laptop’s serial number.

Edit config.cfg and update S1_AGENT_CUSTOMER_ID . Replace tanuki with the username portion (the part before the @ sign or the local-part per RFC2822 ) of your GitLab email address. Replace ABCD1234 with your laptop’s serial number. Verify that the edited variable is formatted correctly with a hyphen separating the GitLab email and serial number. For example, S1_AGENT_CUSTOMER_ID=jdoe@gitlab.com-ABCD1234 .

Edit config.cfg and update S1_AGENT_CUSTOMER_ID .

Replace tanuki with the username portion (the part before the @ sign or the local-part per RFC2822 ) of your GitLab email address.

Replace ABCD1234 with your laptop’s serial number.

Verify that the edited variable is formatted correctly with a hyphen separating the GitLab email and serial number. For example, S1_AGENT_CUSTOMER_ID=jdoe@gitlab.com-ABCD1234 .

Install the package (distro commands may vary). # Fedora 37+ and RPM distributions export S1_AGENT_INSTALL_CONFIG_PATH = ~/.config/sentinelone/config.cfg sudo -E rpm -i --nodigest ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.rpm # Ubuntu 22.04+ and Debian/Ubuntu distributions export S1_AGENT_INSTALL_CONFIG_PATH = ~/.config/sentinelone/config.cfg sudo -E apt install ~/Downloads/sentinelagent.deb

Install the package (distro commands may vary).

Verify that the agent is running in the terminal output. Setting registration token... Registration token successfully set Setting management device type... Device type successfully set Setting customer ID... Customer ID successfully set Starting agent... Agent is running

Verify that the agent is running in the terminal output.

Verify that the systemd service is running. systemctl status sentinelone ● sentinelone.service - Monitor SentinelOne Agent Loaded: loaded (/lib/systemd/system/sentinelone.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled) Active: active (running) since Fri 2023-02-10 09:37:35 CET; 4min 5s ago Process: 298024 ExecStart=/opt/sentinelone/bin/sentinelctl control run (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS) Main PID: 298039 (s1-agent) Status: "Starting agent..." Tasks: 50 Memory: 594.6M (limit: 7.9E) CPU: 1min 19.288s CGroup: /system.slice/sentinelone.service ├─298034 s1-orchestrator "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" ├─298035 s1-network "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" ├─298037 s1-scanner "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" ├─298039 s1-agent "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" " ├─298041 s1-firewall "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" " ├─298043 s1-fanotify "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" " └─298045 s1-perf "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" "" ""

Verify that the systemd service is running.

If you see the previous step outputs, you’re all set! Not working? See to the linux installation error steps on the troubleshooting handbook page.

If you see the previous step outputs, you’re all set!

Not working? See to the linux installation error steps on the troubleshooting handbook page.

Not working? See to the linux installation error steps on the troubleshooting handbook page.
